meta.label: Indian
meta.description: A broad, spice-forward cuisine family spanning regional traditions across the Indian subcontinent. Built around layered aromatics, toasted whole spices, masala blends, legumes and vegetables, yogurt-based marinades, and starches like rice and flatbreads.
meta.subtitle: Masala-centric, aromatic, and highly adaptable across vegetarian and meat-based meals.
heritageNotes: This profile is a practical planner-oriented synthesis of common Indian cooking patterns rather than a single regional canon.
Regional subprofiles below help planners choose different flavor directions and staple sets.
meta.author: ssa_catalog

source.notes: Designed for planner defaults, pantry guidance, and substitution hints for SSA.

overlapsWith.reason: Shared use of warm spices, kebab-style grilling, rice pairings, and yogurt-based sauces in some subregions.
Historical spice trade overlap and shared masala-like blends in coastal contexts.

subprofiles.name: North Indian
subprofiles.signatureNotes: Creamy tomato-onion gravies, tandoor-style roasting, garam masala finishes, wheat flatbreads.

subprofiles.name: South Indian
subprofiles.signatureNotes: Coconut, curry leaves, mustard seed tempering, tamarind and tangy sambar/rasam-style profiles.

subprofiles.name: West Indian
subprofiles.signatureNotes: Gujarati sweet-sour notes, peanut/sesame accents, coastal coconut and seafood traditions.

subprofiles.name: East Indian
subprofiles.signatureNotes: Mustard oil, mustard seed pastes, panch phoron, fish and greens, lighter broths.

notes: Use this profile to generate meal plans that balance a spiced main + starch + quick vegetable/raita/pickle.
The flavor system emphasizes tempering (tadka), masala building, and bright acid finishes.
Ingredient IDs use SSA-style ids (e.g., ing.onion). If your ingredient catalog uses different ids, map them via aliases/resolver.

makeAheadNotes: Keep a ginger_garlic_paste batch for fast weeknight curries.
Pre-cook dal or chickpeas and freeze in 2-cup portions.
Make a basic onion_tomato_masala base and freeze for quick gravies.

saltDefaults.notes: Salt early for onions/tomatoes; finish with a final taste after simmering.

tier0_minimum.description: Starter items to cook basic Indian-style spiced meals with minimal shopping.

tier1_core.description: Core pantry for most weeknight curries, dals, and rice dishes.

tier2_traditional.description: Traditional items enabling deeper regional dishes and authentic finishing layers.

tier3_specialty.description: Specialty items for restaurant-style texture, regional snacks, and advanced techniques.

notes: Tempering (tadka) is a key flavor delivery mechanism: hot fat + whole spices + aromatics poured over dal/veg.
Most gravies start with an onion-ginger-garlic-tomato base, then spices bloom in fat before liquid.
Finish with herbs/acid (lemon, amchur, tamarind) for lift.

dishArchetypes.label: Dal (lentil/legume stew)
dishArchetypes.description: A simmered lentil dish often finished with tadka; served with rice or flatbread.

dishArchetypes.label: Curry with gravy
dishArchetypes.description: Saucy main built from an onion-tomato masala base; can be veg or meat.

dishArchetypes.label: Dry sauté (sabzi)
dishArchetypes.description: Vegetable-forward dish cooked with spices, often drier than curry; great weeknight side.

dishArchetypes.label: Spiced rice (pulao/biryani-style)
dishArchetypes.description: Aromatic rice with whole spices; can be simple pulao or layered biryani patterns.

avoid_or_limit.label: High sugar additions
avoid_or_limit.reason: Use sweeteners sparingly; sweetness is typically balance, not dominant, except in some regional dishes.

proteinSubstitutions.notes: Adjust cook time: breast cooks faster; goat/lamb may need longer simmering; tofu/paneer can be added near the end.
To keep vegetarian, prefer tofu or chickpeas.

sideSubstitutions.notes: If no flatbread, serve over rice or with a simple starch like potatoes.

thickenerSubstitutions.notes: Cashews make creamy gravies; sunflower seeds blend similarly; coconut milk shifts flavor; yogurt must be tempered to prevent curdling.
Besan adds flavor; starches thicken more neutrally—use less and add gradually.

notes: If you lack a masala blend, approximate with cumin + coriander + turmeric + chili + a pinch of cinnamon/clove/cardamom.
If curry leaves are unavailable, increase cilantro and use mustard seed + a small pinch of fenugreek for aroma.

mealTemplates.label: Weeknight Dal + Rice + Quick Salad

components.notes: Cook dal; finish with tadka if possible.
Simple chopped salad with lemon and salt.
Add lemon wedges, raita, or pickle for contrast.

prepNotes: Make extra dal for lunch; it reheats well.

mealTemplates.label: Curry + Roti + Raita

components.notes: Roti is most typical for weeknights.
Raita cools heat and balances spices.

prepNotes: Make a double batch of onion_tomato_masala base and freeze portions.

safetyNotes.label: Prevent yogurt curdling
notes: Temper yogurt with a spoonful of hot sauce before stirring in.
Use full-fat yogurt for better stability.

safetyNotes.label: Whole spice handling
notes: Whole spices can be removed before serving if desired.
Toast whole spices briefly; do not burn them or bitterness develops.

availabilityNotes.note: Curry leaves are distinctive; if unavailable, lean on mustard seed tempering and fresh herbs for aroma.
Asafoetida adds onion-garlic depth; omit or increase aromatics.

hallmarkProcesses.name: Tadka (tempering)
hallmarkProcesses.notes: Heat fat until shimmering, bloom whole spices briefly, add aromatics, then pour over dal or vegetables.

hallmarkProcesses.name: Onion-tomato masala base
hallmarkProcesses.notes: Cook onions until golden, bloom spices in fat, cook tomatoes down until thick and glossy.

equipmentAffinity.notes: Pressure cooker speeds dal and tougher meats. Blender helps smooth gravies (e.g., butter-style sauces).

notes: A small tempering pan (or small skillet) is useful for tadka.
A spice grinder makes fresh masala blends; otherwise use pre-ground.

vegetarian.notes: Many staples are vegetarian (dal, sabzi, rice dishes).

vegan.notes: Use oil instead of ghee; coconut/yogurt alternatives.

gluten_free.notes: Rice and many curries are GF; check spice blends and serve with rice or GF flatbreads.

core.name: Tadka Dal
core.notes: Weeknight staple; dal finished with tempering.

core.name: Chana Masala
core.notes: Chickpea curry with onion-tomato base and warm spices.

core.name: Aloo Gobi
core.notes: Dry sauté of potato and cauliflower with turmeric and cumin.

core.name: Chicken Curry
core.notes: Flexible curry pattern; coconut milk variant leans south/coastal.

core.name: Jeera Rice
core.notes: Cumin-scented rice; pairs with most curries and dals.

typicalFlavorPairings.note: Cool + fresh sides balance heat and richness.
Dal likes acid/bright sides and a starch base.

antiPairings.note: Excess sweetness can flatten spice complexity; keep sweeteners as balance unless regional dish calls for it.

ingredientRules.notes: Use neutral oil for vegan. Coconut oil shifts flavor toward coastal/south notes.
Tamarind is distinct; lemon/amchur provide brightness; vinegar is a last-resort acid.
Blend seeds with water for creamy base; coconut milk adds sweetness; yogurt adds tang.

methodRules.notes: Cook dal and chickpeas by simmering longer; plan extra time and water.

batchFriendly.name: Onion-tomato masala base
batchFriendly.notes: Cook down a large batch and freeze in 1–2 cup portions for quick curries.

batchFriendly.name: Ginger-garlic paste
batchFriendly.notes: Freeze in ice-cube trays for fast aromatics.

batchFriendly.name: Cooked dal base
batchFriendly.notes: Cook and freeze; finish each meal with fresh tadka and acid.

weeknightShortcuts: Use canned chickpeas when time is short; simmer 10 minutes in masala to absorb flavor.
Keep a frozen masala base and add protein/veg + coconut milk/yogurt to finish.
Make jeera rice or plain basmati in a rice cooker while curry simmers.

notes: Planner: prefer pairing a saucy main with a starch and a cooling side (raita/salad).
If pantry lacks whole spices, the system may lean on blends (garam_masala) and ground spices.
